desc:
The entire room is inlaid in obsidian, giving the room a beautiful yet ominous
sparkle. One can't help but gasp in astonishment at the sight, the curiosity
about these people heightened even more. There is however some debris which is
most certainly out of place here, along with a smell that says this room is
still frequented.
name: Obsidian Room

desc:
The tunnel ends abruptly here, the obsidian walls glistening gently. The dank
smell is still present, making your nose wrinkle somewhat. Mostly one can wonder
why the passage ends here in such an illogical spot. Perhaps this tunnel was
made for a more covert and clandestine purpose.
name: Dank Passageway

desc:
Glistening slightly from the moisture condensing on the walls, this tunnel is
not quite as dark as some of the others here. The walls and floor are made of a
very smooth, shiny kind of rock, unlike anything you've seen before. There are
small pools of standing water here and there, causing a stagnant smell to
permeate the environment.
name: Dank Passageway

desc:
This crossing is largely dominated by the huge mural that is on the north wall.
It must be very ancient indeed, as certain scenes are missing, or are simply no
longer able to be seen. While it is indeed beautiful, it also serves to add an
extremely eerie feeling to this whole place. More than ever, one cannot stop
wondering who made this place, and why they are apparently no longer here.
name: Eerie Intersection

desc:
This room is not lit by any ambient light, so if your eyes are not suited to
darkness, you had better light an artificial source. It is somewhat cool in
here, as the room itself is carved into the very hillside. This seems to have
been the conversing room of the dwelling, being fairly large and open. A cracked
tabletop lies on the floor, as well as the charred remains of a chair. The floor
is naturally hard-packed dirt, and a layer of dust covers everything.
name: A Side Room

desc:
This crude shelter seems very sturdy. It is larger than it appeared from
outside, as a second room has been cut into the hillside next to it. The roof
you saw from the outside only covers about half of the area. The place seems to
be uninhabited at the moment, as you see no tell-tale signs of life. It was
probably used by the Dwarves of Moria as a resting spot on their long treks to
the city of Ost-in-Edhil in the west. Another room is to the east.
name: A Crude Stone Shelter

desc:
Abruptly the trail ends here. You are standing before a simple stone dwelling,
that looks abandoned at this time. No windows are present, so you may not peek
inside from here. Only going in the place will give you a clue as to its
purpose. Around you are dark trees looming high above. Since the stone that
constructs the house is so natural looking, it blends well with the hill that
borders it to the east. It would be difficult to spot the house from far away.
name: Before a Stone House

desc:
Darkness prevails as the trail continues to the east and west. The shadows that
obscure much of the area make it difficult to see, but it is possible to discern
some strange tracks on the ground, although its hard to tell just who, or what,
made them. To the west the path seems to end abruptly, and you seem to be able
to make out some sort of old shelter there as well.
name: Shadowed Path

desc:
Here the stone passage makes a corner, branching off in different directions.
The utter darkness makes it too difficult to tell anything else as it seems to
literally eat up any light source that enters its domain. One could easily get
lost in here it seems, but the general layout seems simple enough. Rough hewn
walls are all that is here, along with a few old torch brackets, now empty.
Nothing extraordinary adorns the place.
name: Well-Hewn Tunnel

desc:
The ground here is mostly flat, with a slope rising upwards to the north. Wild
grasses grow in abundance, fed well by the fertile topsoil of these plains.
Small patches of clover litter the countryside, blowing gently in even the
faintest of breezes.
name: South of a Ridge

desc:
Grass covers most of the ground on this section of the ridge, giving good
footing. Small bare patches of rock dot the area to the north, where the ridge
takes a steep turn downwards. A careful person would be able to scale that slope
without risking life or limb.
name: Grassy Ridge in the Plains

desc:
Beds of clover thrive in this sheltered corner, protected from the elements by
ridges to the south and west. The land is mostly flat, with small rises which
could not possibly be considered hills. An almost sheer cliff to the west makes
progress in that direction impossible. A steep and rocky slope to the south
should make a vigorous but not overly difficult climb.
name: In the Corner of Two Small Ridges

desc:
The ground here is soft and green, covered in a thick carpet of healthy grass.
The ridge to the west has a steep slope which would be difficult but not
impossible to scale. Some stones have fallen down at its base, creating a small
pile of rubble.
name: Verdant Plains by a Ridge

desc:
Healthy, green grass grows in abundance on this flat stretch of land, seemingly
at odds with the mountains looming far to the east. Small patches of bright
flowers liven up the landscape. In the distance to the north and south, small
hills can be seen rising above the surroundings.
name: Verdant Plains

desc:
This large flat area lies in a depression. The slopes of the ridge to the west
are covered with a mixture of wild flowers and sturdy grasses. A ditch created
by water runoff from one of the steeper parts of the western ridge fans out at
the base with sand which was carried down.
name: Base of the Ridge

desc:
The top of this small hill sits over twenty feet higher than the surrounding
countryside. A ridge running east-west intersects with the hill some ten feet
lower than the crown, which is covered in a thick layer of grass. The sides of
the hill slope down at a moderate angle to the north and south, making for an
easy climb.
name: On Top of the Small Rise

desc:
Looking about you, you can find absolutely no evidence that this was once one of
the more populated regions of Arda, home of both Elf and Dwarf, who together
brought more glories to this land than it has ever seen, or is ever likely to
see again. Indeed it is not hard to believe, looking at the beauty of these
parts, it's just that there is no sign of civilization anywhere.
name: Forested Slope

desc:
You walk lightly through these hills, with tall grasses and wildflowers all
around you. The flora is so healthy in fact, that the grass springs immediately
upright following your footfalls, leaving barely any sign of recent travel. The
hills are well above most of the nearby terrain, but the grass is so high in
places that it is sometimes hard to even see a foot ahead. East is a higher
unclimbable rocky hill.
name: Hills of Hollin

desc:
You are on a fairly high mountain ridge layered with course rocks and tiny
pebbles. To the east is a valley that winds its way northward. At the end of
that valley you see a large stone formation resembling a crown.
name: On a Rocky Mountain Ridge

desc:
You are walking on a small path at the bottom of a valley with steep slopes. The
valley seems to wind its way northwards, like a great serpent between the
mountain ridges. Resembling the skin of the serpent, showing all the differnt
hues of brown and green on its scales, lie the valley slopes, clad with
occasional trees and bushes.
name: White Valley

desc:
Covered with a tall, green blanket of grass, your stroll along this ridge is
quite enjoyable. Hot and sweaty from the climb, you enjoy the light breeze that
sweeps across this mountain ridge.
name: A Low Mountain Ridge

desc:
These plains are tucked between a stream to the north and east and a ridge to
the south. The plains overlook a deep valley to the west - a deep furrow in the
earth that has a somewhat unnatural look.
name: Plains along the Stream

desc:
Still the valley goes on, and now a chilly wind starts to blow, blowing right
through your clothes and freezing your skin. You bite your teeth together as a
sign of resilience. Still, the cold is a strange kind of cleaning feeling; much
like the beauty of this place is a cold beauty; as lifeless and hard as rocks.
name: White Valley

desc:
As you turn a hill sticking far out from the western side of the valley, a huge
stone formation comes to view. It resembles a crown, seven peaks rising at the
end of the valley. You quickly decide to go nearer and study the crown up close.
name: Bend in the Valley

desc:
Before you is the most impressive stone formation you have ever seen; a great
crown of rock seems to have been set at the end of the valley, leaving you
standing at the king's throne. A steep climb it is, but you could probably make
it. You're eager to see what hides in the middle of those peaks.
name: At the Base of the Natural Crown

desc:
You are in the middle of a giant stone-basin, its floor gently sloping into
walls, smooth and flawless except for a single stair spiraling out from where
you stand. At perfect intervals around the rim of the basin, seven peaks rise,
giving you the impression of standing in the middle of a king's crown. This must
indeed be the work of Aule himself, a gift to the people whose father he is.
name: A Natural Crown

desc:
Everything is peaceful in these hills, and you walk easily through them with the
wind blowing lightly through your hair. The landscape is the very picture of
serenity, with vibrant colour nearly everywhere you look, from the sky above to
the wildflowers on the ground. The only unsettling element of this otherwise
perfect setting the a shadow which seems to be on this land, perhaps cast by the
rocky peak to the east.
name: Hills of Hollin

desc:
The hills stretch far in any direction, and in fact you cannot see much else.
There are several forested areas however, and the glimmering silvery strip of a
tributary of the Sirannon, long dried. Naturally you can't help but notice to
the east the mighty mountains of Moria, known in the elven-tongue as Caradhras,
Celebdil, and Fanuidhol.
name: Hills of Hollin

desc:
This is a tributary of the Sirannon, bringing at some water to the waterbed
north. The shallow bed left behind by the waterway runs east towards the
mountains, and is lost on the western horizon. To the west is a cliff over the
river, and hills lie in all other directions.
name: Tributary of Sirannon

desc:
Still the river twists and turns, having shaped the surrounding land to it's
will. This particular bend in the river is to the west and north, and is still
bordered by hills to the south, which are not quite accessible from here. Coming
from the mountains to the south-east, a river adds quite some water to the
streambed, which makes a bit more alive than it was further east of here.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
If you look slowly around, it is possible to see for miles and miles in any
direction here. Looking to the west trying to take in every single detail, you
could strain your eyes and hope for a glimpse of the ancient city of
Ost-in-Edhil, which has lain in ruin for thousands of years. Try as you might,
however, you cannot find it. Probably it is too far away to be seen from here.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
Seemingly neverending are these hills which grace the land known to Elves of old
as Eregion. Various colourful flowers and bushes crown the green landscape in
full bloom, as you witness some of Eru's finest work on display in front of you.
The hills continue smoothly to both the north and west, and off to the south and
east you can make out an old track.
name: Hills of Hollin

desc:
This track continues along with no end to it in sight. To the east though, it
seems to make a turn to the north, separating from its long-time travelling
partner, the Sirannon. You notice all sorts of different types of terrain in
these parts, from the grassy hills and forest to the north of the road, to the
slightly more desolate hills to the south of it. For some reason however, the
more desolate hills look somewhat more hospitable.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
You wander now in the uneven bed of the Sirannon, a stream which once made its
path known across Arda, but has long since decreased its flow. From here you can
continue in the streambed, or enter some of the lovely hills which grace the
land of Nan-Eregdos. To the north, you can even climb up onto an ancient road,
and see where that may take you.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
The trail still goes on before you, and though it is comfortable, it constantly
has you watching out for roots or brambles to stumble in. To truly enjoy the
scenery, you have to stop and watch for a while.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The path continues to wind its way from the gates of Moria to Ost-in-Edhil.
Walking is comfortable, and you start to hum softly to yourself as you skip
along.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
A long time ago, this was a great road, the main route between the elven city
Ost-in-Edhil, and the dwarven dwellings of Khazad-dum. Now it is little more
than a trail, overgrown with bushes and brambles.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
At this point, the path bends in a long, smooth curve. Knowing the dangers of
straying from the road, you decide to follow the path; either east towards
Moria, or south towards the city of Ost-in-Edhil.
name: Bend in Road

desc:
The road continues to stretch for as far as the eye can see. You let out a heavy
sigh as you realise the long journey ahead. To the south you can see the dry
riverbed of the Sirannon.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
A fairly straight trail lies before you; east towards Moria, and west towards
Ost-in-Edhil. You relax and begin to enjoy the comfortable stroll, as you
realise the long journey ahead.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The trail bends again here, winding its way north towards the gates of Moria.
From here you can barely see the high peaks of the Misty Mountains to your east.
To the south lies the once flowing river Sirannon.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
Still heading north and south, the road enters a terrain of much diversity.
Hills, forests, river water, and open flat-lands all grace Eregion in this
sector. The rise of a large hill of rock just north of you catches your eye. A
barely visible path seems to skirt it at the near side, seeming to go around it,
to where the water flows quietly by. You can still see the riverbed from here,
carrying the small stream westwards toward Ost-in-Edhil.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
Still heading north-south here, the road begins to pass a large rock mound to
your east. Not a small mound, more like a small hill of barren boulders, it
stands firm, unyielding. Hills lie on your west, with trees and holly growing in
abundance. The air is pure, and the sound of water running close by is
comforting. The water you surmise, is that of the diminished River Sirannon,
winding from the West gate of Moria to its destination of Ost-in-Edhil, far to
the west.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
Leading north-south now, the wide dirt track passes out of view of the riverbed.
As it was a comforting sight and sound, you hope to see it again on your trek
down this road. The rock formation is unclimbable from this spot, yet you think
there is an easier slope on the far side of it. Some small hills lie just west
of you, looking gentle and rolling. Trees adorn the hills, though smaller and
less dense than the trees of the forest to your north. The now familiar holly
bush still grows in plentiful proportions.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
You walk on through this channel-like riverbed finding at every turn mud, mud,
and more mud. The riverbed is making its final turn around the forest to your
southeast, making a smooth curve around it. To the north you can see the road
that connects Ost-in-Edhil to Moria.
name: Bend in the Riverbed

desc:
The Sirannon is still far from full capacity, and the small stream seems dwarfed
by the riverbed in which it is flowing. The Hollin Road still runs alongside the
northern bank, while to the south is a lush and dense forest.
name: Sirannon Riverbed

desc:
You try to avoid the mud in the bottom of the riverbed, but you find it quite
strenuous to keep walking on the rather steep slopes. Your boots are already
muddy all over, and you rapidly tire of that annoying splish-splosh sound.
name: Sirannon Riverbed

desc:
In every direction save north, trees have taken root in the rich soil. To the
north, the channel that now houses the shrunken Sirannon can be made out through
a gap in the trees. The land is quiet - the stream's burble is practically the
only sound.
name: Forest along the Riverbed

desc:
You are walking leisurely along the shrunken Sirannon to the north. Here, on top
of the river slopes, the birds sing and the squirrels scamper around, harvesting
nuts for the winter. You take care not to move to close to the bank, as you
might fall down the rather steep slopes.
name: Forest along the Riverbed

desc:
The riverbed takes a great swing around the forest to your south, before it
continues on eastwards. When the river was more active, it dug itself deep into
the ground, so following the riverbed is like walking a small valley.
name: Bend in the Riverbed

desc:
To the east, the riverbed takes a turn toward the north, passing beyond view. On
the northern bank, the ancient Hollin Road runs - a testament to the skill of
the Noldor and Dwarves at working stone, for it is still in fair shape after the
passage of four millenia.
name: Sirannon Riverbed

desc:
Up ahead, the riverbed takes another bend to the north. Colourful rocks are
strewn about, smoothened by the river's passage. Occasionally, the rocks reflect
the sunlight, making them especially beautiful.
name: Sirannon Riverbed

desc:
You are wandering along the edge of a lush, dense forest. The trees towering
above you prevent most of the sunlight from reaching you, shielding you from the
hot summer sun. To the north you can see the almost dry riverbed of the
Sirannon; to the south you can barely see the peaks of the Misty Mountains.
name: Sirannon Forest

desc:
The forest stretches on all around you, and huge trees prevent you from seeing
very far. Not that you need to see far; the forest is beautiful enough, if a bit
too quiet. Long ago the elves abode in this land; but the trees do not remember
them, nor do the animals; but the rocks do, singing their quiet songs.
name: Sirannon Forest

desc:
You enjoy your stroll along this forest because the trees provide much needed
shade from the cruel, hot rays of the sun. Always aware that you may not be the
only inhabitants in this forest, you remain quite cautious.
name: Sirannon Forest

desc:
You've dared the tree, but will you dare the jump? It might be your only chance
of getting to the other side of the crevice, but it's well over two yards, and
you haven't exactly got much running space.
name: In the Tree

desc:
You are carefully walking along the edge of the Misty Mountains making sure each
step has a firm footing; one slip and you may end up falling into the deep
crevice to your west. To the east the mountains become steeper and steeper.
name: Edge of a Mountain

desc:
You are on a very steep, rocky incline. Unable to head back south, you can
either head north towards the Misty Mountains or west to a small knoll along the
river.
name: A Steep Slope

desc:
The water creates a tumult of icy spray as it speeds along its course, fish can
be seen jumping to avoid crashing against the rocks. To the north lies a steep
slope; going up would be rough, coming down would be impossible.
name: Tributary of the Sirannon

desc:
The river bends here, curving its way from the east to the north eating its way
through the rocks. As the water changes course, a large, calm pool is formed.
name: Tributary of the Sirannon

desc:
The water beats itself against the rocks, polishing their surfaces to a
glistening slipperiness. The sound of churning water echos through the crisp
air.
name: Tributary of the Sirannon

desc:
A small waterfall tickling down the Misty Mountains is the source of this river.
From here the water flows westward down the rocky mountain side, carrying with
it fallen leaves from the nearby forest.
name: Tributary of the Sirannon

desc:
The ground slopes down again, and you walk into an ocean of mist. You can see
your nearest surroundings, which suprisingly enough, consist mainly of trees.
The trees are dead and black as ebon, though, their branches writhing tentacles
that seem ready to trap anyone who comes near them. You try to keep your
distance to the trees, and that leads you eastwards, deeper into the forest.
name: Misty Forest

desc:
Due to some unknown reason, you have walked deeper inside this forest. The trees
almost seem to spread in various patterns, leading you to where they want you to
go. Suddenly, you are looking out over a round lake somewhere deep in this
forest, the lake giving away a pale reflection of the sunlight.
name: Deep in the Misty Forest

desc:
Floating on the pale surface of this shadowy lake, it is easy to see this lake
lies within the middle of a misty forest. The trees surround this place in all
directions. Only to the south does there seem to be way ashore. Elsewhere the
brambles and trees simply stand too thick. Perhaps this lake is hiding a secret
beneath its surface.
name: Shadowy Lake

desc:
The trail abruptly ends here, leaving you quite disappointed. For there seems to
only be one choice left, and that is to head back. Realizing that a fall from
this elevation would be fatal, you are careful not to stand too close to the
edge of the trail.
name: End of Path

desc:
The carved-out path slowly slopes downard along the side of the mountain. As you
drag your tired feet along the rocky, dust-ridden trail, dust flies in your
face, stinging your eyes, and leaving a mouthful of dust granules.
name: Rocky Mountain Path

desc:
A crackling fire is burning in the middle of this fairly large cave, giving
faint light, and making flickering shadows on the smooth walls. The cave is warm
and well isolated; it seems like the dwarves' work. One wall is stacked with
fire-wood, by the other there are many chests, probably for food. At the far
wall stand a table and some chairs, and in the inner corner are some improvised
beds.
name: An Old Guarding Cave

desc:
Awe strikes you as you slowly proceed into this dully lit chamber, the light
coming from two candles situated at either side of the square room. In the exact
middle of it lies a short stone coffin, its sides and top beautifully ornamented
with elaborate carvings. A slab is raised on one of the coffin's ends, like a
tombstone, with writings on it.
name: Dwarven Tomb

desc:
A snow covered slope leads up towards even thinner air; air that is flowing down
the slope like an invisible glacier freezing your body. As your mind pushes your
body forward, your body rebels threatening not to take another step.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Footprints in the ice signal that a large biped has passed through this area
since the last snowfall. The tracks lead to the eastern cliff face, and appear
to ascend the impossible surface.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The snow here is more ice then snow, revealing to those who use a little thought
that it has not snowed here for years. What was it so long ago that lifted these
mountains?
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Ironically, even though you sit upon one of the tallest mountains of Arda, your
view is very dull, merely a few ice covered rocks. In an effort to keep warm,
you try to imagine the warmth of your homeland.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The last of the trees lie far below you now. What greets you is the dismal sight
of rock and ice blending into a glassy grey. You begin to dream of days filled
with better greetings.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
The familiar sight of ice and rock fills your view, jagged slopes and icy
pitfalls. Far to the east a large chunk of ice rises above the other mountain
tops.
name: Misty Mountains

desc:
Here, the ground rises sharply as if to stop you from venturing further into the
Misty Mountains; though the climb is quite steep and treacherous, you decide to
attempt this feat due to your sense of adventure.
name: A Steep Mountain Slope

desc:
You continue to make your way up the mountain; you are careful not to lose your
footing, one slip may be fatal. To the east lies the heart of the Misty
Mountains.
name: Up a Mountain

desc:
The forest suddenly changes character; the growth is so wild that it becomes
nearly impenetrable. The rest of the forest is very pleasant, and so are the
inhabitants, at least most of them.
name: Edge of Forest

desc:
You stand in a bowl-shaped hollow in the moors, a rather distinct element in the
terrain; and that is exactly why you picked walking here. Yet you are back to
where you started; from here every direction seems the same. You stare
cheerlessly at the slopes around you, hoping to find a path or some other
landmark.
name: Hollow in the Moors

desc:
A lush layer of grass blankets the moors, leaving an impressive image in your
mind. Relaxed and at peace, you feel as if you could stay here forever; but as a
true adventurer, you decide to continue your journey.
name: Green Moors

desc:
The path disappears altogether, and you are at the point of returning when you
suddenly spot some kind of strange stone portal set between two ridges. An arm
of mist seems to be reaching out of that portal, but the mist dissolves in the
air when it gets any distance away from the portal.
name: Path in the Moors

desc:
Still the path winds on, leading you deeper and deeper into the moors. When the
path is at its higher points, you can catch a glimpse of the mountains to the
south. Suddenly, you lose sight of it again, and you start to wonder whether it
was all just a trick of the eye.
name: Path in the Moors

desc:
The stream bends from the east to the north here. To the south lie the moors.
Stories tell that the moors are haunted, and children and grown-ups alike are
loath to go there. If you're lost on the moors, it's said, you have lost
yourself.
name: A Bend in the Stream

desc:
A small stream pours forth through a crack in the cliff to the east, merrily
cascading downward about twenty feet. The water is frigid - it must originate
somewhere in the Misty Mountains to the east. The stream flows away westward
into the lands of Eregion.
name: Beneath a Small Waterfall

desc:
This small stream flows out of the mountains to the east - its water still
pristine. To the west, it joins with another stream that is flowing down the
middle of a wide streambed. To the south, the stream continues.
name: Small Stream

desc:
At this point the riverbed splits; you are left with the choice of either
heading west, along a path soiled with footprints, or east, along a path not
often traveled.
name: Fork in the River

desc:
The riverbed abruptly bends east, and you bang your head against the stone as
you try to change directions yourself as well. You look up towards the sun and
delight in having the few beams that can reach you warm you up a bit, until you
hit your head against the stone again. Ouch.
name: Bend in the Riverbed

desc:
The riverbed bends here, curving downwards from the north to the west. Further
down, the riverbed begins to run parallel with the Hollin road, the main path
that connects Moria to Ost-in-Edhil.
name: Bend in the Riverbed

desc:
A thin layer of soft grass covers most of the rolling ground, except for some
large rocks clad with moss. Aside from these, the moors contain few or no
landmarks to speak of. Animal life is scarce here, maybe a worm or two
slithering on through the grass, or some strange bird flying high above you.
name: Green Moors

desc:
The slopes of the moors get steeper, and the vegetation scarcer, as it turns
into a more hilly landscape. Chilly, but refreshing winds blow upon the
hilltops, bearing in them the legacy of the mountains far east. Somewhat north
runs the riverpath of the now dry Sirannon river, carrying that same legacy.
name: Green Moors

desc:
The darkness here is all-consuming, so that even a bright light can seem to fail
at times, leaving you using your hands to find your way. Occasionally an
unfamiliar noise breaks through the gloomy silence, echoing eerily all around
you, its source impossible to locate.
name: An Underground Cavern

desc:
Around every corner is nothing but darkness. Your sense of direction almost
fails you (while your sense of time is long lost), but you think you are walking
in a fairly straight southwest direction. You start to wonder about how many
passages and side tunnels must have eluded you; but you won't let it distract
you as you walk on, determined to find...what?
name: An Underground Cavern

desc:
With each passing moment you find it harder to keep calm in this consuming
darkness, which only becomes worse when your feeble light tries to penetrate it,
casting flickering shadows on the walls. An inner voice reminds you that a
cavern such as this is crowded with silent hunters, and that a probable aim for
those hunters would be you.
name: An Underground Cavern

desc:
You have never heard of this place, let alone heard its name; but it comes
creeping upon you as you step into a system of caverns, each cave entrance is
like a giant fiend's gaping maw, each stalactite and stalagmite ready to strike
at you. The flickering light of your torch does not reveal much. As for your
sense of smell, something foul has left a thick and stale stench; and it's not
far away. The only reason why you didn't run back out is that the way behind you
is blocked.
name: An Underground Cavern

desc:
The crudely wrought walls reach out to touch you, and when you bend away from
them, they eat your light instead. You start to tremble as you hear footsteps
coming from every direction; or perhaps these noises are the result of your
overactive imagination.
name: An Underground Cavern

desc:
A narrow walkway winds its way westward towards the edge of the mountain; you
can see it split into two paths up ahead, making way for a pile of twigs,
branches, and sticks oddly placed neatly in a circular fashion.
name: Out on a Ledge

desc:
Climbing up this tall peak was a great accomplishment on your part; you are
quite tired from the exhausting climb so you decide to rest here for a while.
While enjoying the scenery, you notice that the southern river in the valley
below originates from here.
name: High Mountain Peak

desc:
You are swimming around in the clear but dark, and, of course, extremely cold
(and wet!) waters of this lake. The rock bottom seems nothing out of the
ordinary to you, neither are any mermaids paddling around here. If this won't
get you a cold, what will?
name: In the Shadowy Lake

desc:
This natural cave must be some kind of an air pocket, at least you can breathe
freely. But the air won't last too long, though, and you suddenly wonder how
that shape deep in the cave could have survived here. Then you realise that it
may not have a life to lose...
name: Strange Cave

desc:
You are perched on a tiny ledge in a deep crevice. The sides of the crevice are
nearly vertical with few to none hand holds. While it might be possible to climb
out, the extremely sharp rocks which litter the floor of the crevice will make
it quite painful if you fail. Some fifty yards below you lies the bottom of the
crevice.
name: A Very Deep Crevice

desc:
The river bends around this small knoll. Though it looked steep, you have
decided to climb it. Among the few stout trees that grow here are patches of
flowers and berries. Especially the holly bush, giving this region its name,
Hollin, dominates the undergrowth.
name: A Small Knoll

desc:
You are standing at the bottom of a very deep crevice. Fear envelops you as you
see sharp rocks along the edge. Those who foolishly tried to descend the crevice
met their fate.
name: At the Bottom of the Crevice

desc:
The sides of the mountains rise up to the east and west, cleverly hiding this
path. Even the snow has not found this place and the ground can be seen to be
undisturbed.
name: Down a Steep Slope

desc:
A buzz in your ear reminds you that there are other fauna besides fish that
lives near the river. An itch reminds you how annoying they can be, as you
glance around looking for the culprit.
name: Tributary of the Sirannon

desc:
Floating down the river with you is a rather disconcerting sight; a dead fish
with its belly upturned is attracting flies and crawdads. As you get closer the
smell pierces your nose.
name: Tributary of the Sirannon

desc:
On the side of the river the water flows backwards making movement upstream much
easier for those using boats. Tiny fish can be seen moving within the weeds that
cover the bottom.
name: Tributary of the Sirannon

desc:
You brace youself as you prepare to meet the little waterfall that the water
drags you towards. A two foot drop may not seem like much when on land but to
see the water foam as it heads over the drop brings fear to your heart.
name: Tributary of the Sirannon

desc:
The canyon walls loom to the south and west, a possible source of falling
boulders. You note the presence of many large rocks near the bottom of the
cliff-side.
name: Tributary of the Sirannon

desc:
To the east lies a gloomy shore, the sight of which chills your soul and yet
beckons you with a promise of solid ground. You ponder as images of the beast
which may roam the area fill your mind.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
The smell of rotten vegetation fills the air combining with the strong smell of
fish and mud. On the bank can be seen cakes of dry mud forming a mosaic of
fissures along the edge.
name: Tributary of the Sirannon

desc:
Your way eastwards is blocked by a deep crevice, over which once spanned a stone
bridge. The bridge must have fallen into the crevice a long time ago; now only
the remnants of its ends remain. You are glad to find evidence of inhabitants,
however ancient, but the whole place also gives a spooky feeling.
name: By the Crevice

desc:
This tall ridge towers high above this region, casting a dark shadow to the
west. Here, you can see a river flowing from the base of the Misty Mountains
westward. The trail along this ridge heads north and east.
name: On a Mountain Ridge

desc:
You carefully follow a game trail along this ridge deeper and deeper into the
mountains. You are very careful not to stray far from the path because of the
loose shale slopes; a rock avalanche could start if disturbed.
name: A Mountain Ridge

desc:
The forest seems to thicken to your immediate east; on the other hand, to the
west seems to be a clearing before the river. Otherwise, this forest dominates
the scene, its huge trees and pleasant flowers give it a tranquil feel. The
terrain becomes steeper as you approach the mountain ridge to your south.
name: Sirannon Forest

desc:
Suddenly, you are out of the forest, and standing on a little clearing,
bordering on the dried out riverbed that the Sirannon has become. Still, even
with the absence of the stream, this place radiates peace and beauty. Patches of
flowers in the hues of red and blue and yellow create a soft and lush carpet for
you to lie down on. On the southern side of the clearing is a huge boulder,
creating cool shade for the hot summer days.
name: A Clearing

desc:
Large rocks are placed on regular intervals here, creating a ford across the
small river. The stones are huge, and you wonder what party of stout men or
dwarves or elves could have put them there. Anyway, there's not much need for
them now, only perhaps as a place for children to play.
name: Old Ford in the River

desc:
The riverbed continues to wind its way westward towards Ost-in-Edhil. To the
east lies a now pretty useless ford, only a fairly impressive stone formation
remain in its place. To the north lies the Hollin Road, a trail leading to
Moria.
name: Sirannon Riverbed

desc:
The game trail ends here; your only choice is to climb back down towards a
clearing in the Sirannon Forest to the north or a river to the south. You let
out a heavy sigh of relief as you realise that your journey down would be much
easier than your journey up.
name: A Mountain Ridge

desc:
Shadows of the mountains darken this place, and their slopes echo and amplify
the few sounds to be heard. Now, each footstep splashing in the water is almost
comforting in its familiarity. Yet even here a few small bushes cling to the
banks of the river, growing despite the shadow.
name: On the Banks of a River

desc:
A gigantic fireplace in the southeastern corner dominates this room. The floor
in front of the fireplace is now black with ash, piled up through years of
decay. The rest of the room is only gray with the thick dust- layer lying as a
carpet over the broken and rotted furniture.
name: The Restroom

desc:
Much smaller than the last one, this room contains almost nothing but a desk
with a book spread out upon it. The air is stale, and a thick layer of dust
covers every inch of the room; it seems like centuries since anyone has set
their foot here.
name: A Hidden Chamber

desc:
This room is very simple and sparsely equipped, yet it gives you the feeling
that many a great dwarf has spent his hours here. A wash basin still stands on a
nearly crumbled wooden table beside a long since rotted bed. A thick layer of
dust covers the entire room. A book lies on the bed, and suprisingly enough, it
still contains some readable passages.
name: The Silent Chamber

desc:
Standing on this fairly high ridge, you have a wonderful view of the area; to
the north lies a dense forest and to the south lies a deep crevice, preventing
you from going higher up the Misty Mountains. You notice a tall tree on the
southern edge of the ridge.
name: A Mountain Ridge
Sirannon Forest

desc:
For some reason, this feels like the exact middle of this forest, its very
essence seems to hide here. The huge trees forming the grove give a feeling of
tranquility combined with respectfulness, not to mention some strange kind of
watchfulness.
name: An Oak Grove

desc:
This small rise separates the riverbed of the Sirannon and the Hollin Road.
Sloping upwards only a few meters above the small stream, it is just high enough
to give a scenic view down the length of the river. Holly bushes cover the hill
with the same lush green leaves that adorn most other parts of the region.
Looking north, you see a large rock formation. Looking like a table, it seems to
have been there for many many years.
name: A Gentle Rise

desc:
The flat area you are in is very scenic and peaceful. The riverbed can be seen
to your east and south, giving the field an island-like atmosphere. Just
northwards, the flats continue, but turning more rocky and sandy. A massive rock
pile to the north, almost a small hill, makes its presence known by jutting many
meters in the air. Green grass and small flowers sprinkle the field, as well as
some bushes of holly. Fresh tracks in the grass alert you that this is where the
local animals come to graze, forage, and get water.
name: A Riverside Field

desc:
This is the bed where once the Sirannon flowed on its journey from the gates of
Moria to Belegaer, the Great Sea. Something has cut off most of the water supply
farther upstream, for the volume of flow is significantly less than the size of
the streambed would indicate.
name: Riverbed of the Sirannon

desc:
The river takes a more southernly course here, bordering some hills to the
southeast. The Hollin Road at this point leaves the riverbed for a bit, making a
swing to the north, and rejoining the river again later. The water is still
cold, bearing its legacy from the great mountains. The swift flowing stream that
was once here, is now represented by only the tiniest of trickles.
name: Riverbed of the Sirannon

desc:
A pebble strewn beach lies before you, gently leading down to the riverbed of
the Sirannon. The small trickle of water is bending south and east here, flowing
downstream toward its inevitable meeting with the Glanduin. Just to the north,
the rocky mound rises up, making a large table-top formation. A large crack has
opened in the rock face, and water running through it has carved a fair sized
niche out of it.
name: A Rocky Riverbank

desc:
Just east of the Hollin Road, this path is little more than a dirty, matted-down
grass trail. Used by travellers as a quick way to the edge of the river, the
track also leads around the side of the rocks unseen from the main road. Not
terribly high, only about twenty feet, the jutting rock looks plain and made of
granite. A few sparkles glinting off its surface make you wonder if it contains
any mithril, the precious silver metal mined by Dwarves.
name: Path around a Rock

desc:
You are in the forest of East Eregion. Many elm trees tower above your head,
blocking out the light. Although there is no underbrush, it is still very hard
to travel because of the huge, twisted roots of the elm trees. East the forested
hills are interupted by a strip of earth, the Hollin Road.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
You have reach the limit of the forest as it skirts a large mound of rock. This
side of the rock-face is almost vertical, making climbing it unwise. There does
however, seem to be a gentler slope up the far side of it. You hear the sound of
water trickling to the west, though the rocks momentarily block your view of it.
Eastwards the forest continues toward the Hollin Road in the distance.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
Paralelling the dense forest to the east, this grassy hill runs north-south
between rough mounts. Lush vegetation is all around you, made possible by both
the fertile soil in the area, and also by the plentiful amount of water here.
Animals are using the pool to bathe in, clean their food in, and to drink from.
The hills to your west are inaccessible from here, as the decay of rocks forming
the hillside have made unstable cliffs.
name: Grassy Hill

desc:
You are walking in a most fertile hill. It passes between two higher ranges of
hills, and winds down to meet the Hollin Road to the south. It goes further
north as well, leading into northern Eregion and eventually into the lands of
Rhudaur. A small stream is winding in tandem with the trail you are on, and
gives the air a fresh smell as it tumbles along. The stream backs up in places,
thanks to the industrious work of the local beavers. Their dams have created a
series of pools along the stretch of the stream.
name: Grassy Hill

desc:
This thinly wooded forest lies miles off the Hollin Road, which is located to
both the south and east. More of the same terrain is to your north, leading into
a thicker woodland. The ground is soft due to the fallen leaves, and rustles
softly under your feet. Holly bushes grow wherever they are given a chance,
bright green in some places, darker in others. Rocks are scattered among the
foliage, forcing you to either climb over them or go around.
name: Woody Hills

desc:
The forest is growing thick here, and is even denser to the east, where it is
bathed by a river flowing from the mountains. You hear some birds singing,
probably talking about the marvel of the past. Celebrimbor is gone, but the land
did not forget him.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
You are in a forested hill, three miles west of the tallest hill in the area. As
you get closer to the hill, you see that fallen trees cover the hill side. A
storm has recently hit the mound, destroying the vegetation in its path. From
the top of that hill you will have the opportunity to examine the whole area.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
After the easy climb up from the niche, you find yourself atop a flat plateau of
solid rock. Though not very high, the view of surrounding lands is incredible.
Almost every aspect of Eregion presents itself to you from this vantage point.
Below you to the south and east, the riverbed and the Hollin Road wind in tandem
to destinations afar. The north contains holly covered hills and orchards, along
with thick forests that wind eastward.
name: The Rock Top

desc:
Not big enough to be considered a full-fledged cave, this niche is only about
three feet wide and is enclosed by a small overhang from the top of the pile.
One side of the crevice makes for easy access to the peak of the rocks summit.
Just to the south is a grassy area off the side of the riverbed. The surface of
the rock around you is unremarkable, only growing a thin stringy moss called
lichen.
name: Crevice in the Rock

desc:
The river is not flowing with any significant volume, you can see from the marks
on the banks that it has been much greater in earlier times. Around the river
grow many Holly trees, giving the ancient Sindarin name to this region, Hollin.
Now it is called Eregion, and is still a peaceful, comfortable land, in strong
contrast to the looming Hithaeglir, The Misty Mountains.
name: Riverbed of the Sirannon

desc:
Lush indeed are these hills, supported by the endless supply of water from the
large amounts of rainfall which grace this land with its kiss of life. All of
the local terrain looks much the same, in fact, and everything seems full of
floral life. Oddly enough however, there is an absolute silence in these parts,
although you can plainly see a variety of wildlife that you would expect to make
more noise.
name: Holly Covered Hills

desc:
Lush indeed are these hills, supported by the endless supply of water from the
large amounts of rainfall which grace this land with its kiss of life. All of
the local terrain looks much the same, in fact, and everything seems full of
floral life. Oddly enough however, there is an absolute silence in these parts,
although you can plainly see a variety of wildlife that you would expect to make
more noise.
name: Holly Covered Hills

desc:
You are travelling among lower hills in an area called Eregion. The lands have
become greener and warmer. Far in the west you see some vast plains, while the
misty mountains block your view to the east. To the north and the south you see
a region of heavy foliage block your way.
name: A Small Mound

desc:
The ground has mostly dried up, but there are still a few bog plants left
growing here. To the west the ground becomes much firmer and the land rises.
East the mountain begins.
name: A Dried Out Bog

desc:
This section is quite well intact. The trail looks as though it has been built
by professionals with a high standard of work. Perhaps the Dwarves or Elves of
old are responsible for it. It is known that there were ancient trading routes
which passed this way.
name: Decrepit Mountain Trail

desc:
You struggle past the nasty knee-high bracken and occasionally stumble over the
boulders that it conceals. Cursing every single painful step you might begin to
wonder what you are doing in this cold, inhospitable place.
name: Decrepit Mountain Trail

desc:
The trail is level and contours round slightly to the south. With each mile it
turns southward the shadow of Caradhras slowly retreats and the true size of
Celebdil is noticed.
name: Decrepit Mountain Trail

desc:
You are shadowed by the towering peaks of Caradhras and Celebdil. The trail
looks as though in the past a great deal of work has been put into it. But now
the stones and boulders that once made a smooth travelling surface have been
ripped up and are difficult to negotiate. The wind howls through the valley as
if accelerated somehow by the forces of the mountains.
name: Decrepit Mountain Trail

desc:
You are travelling along a region of very rough high ground. Everything else in
this area is the same. There are just hills, outcrops and more countryside. A
shrill chatter breaks the monotony from time to time, making you wonder what
caused it. Looking around the clumps of rock, you see a small mammal about the
size of a beaver. It seems to like darting among the rocks, calling its unique
cry.
name: Mountainous Terrain

desc:
You are climbing along a ledge high up on one of the cliff faces of a peak. On
your west side there is a large rock face. Looking down over the east side of
the ledge you see the Great Walls of Moria. The drop is vast and the walls look
very spectacular from this angle. Far below you there have been many ancient
battles fought over the Gates of Moria and many brave warriors have died.
name: A Rocky Ledge

desc:
The climb takes a sharp turn south and west and becomes very exposed. The plant
life is getting less and less the higher you climb. The ridge looks like it may
have been travelled recently.
name: A Steep Rocky Spur

desc:
You are at the start of a long steep ridge. The footing is good and the plant
life is interspersed with patches of rocks. It would be to your advantage not to
stray from the path as the drops either side might be hazardous for your health.
name: A Steep Rocky Spur

desc:
This is the highest point in this area. You have a clear view of the three
highest mountains in this range. Celebdil is the closest and lies to your east.
It is overshadowed by the mighty Caradhras to its north. To your north-east you
can see the mighty Redhorn Valley climbing up over Caradhras.
name: A Mountain Summit

desc:
This is a tricky little bit to negotiate. You have to climb down a few feet past
a small outcrop then traverse along a ridge with a scary drop below. Let's face
it though you are probably used to the mountains by now.
name: Mountainous Terrain

desc:
You are on the east side of the mountain and as far as you can see there is
scree. In the wet scree such as this could be trecherous. The summit of the
mountain is to your west. The rest of Celebdil can be seen to the east where on
the summit a tower stands.
name: A Scree Slope

desc:
You sense the water is deep, with something sinister lurking below. Every now
and then a few bubbles will cause dark ripples on the otherwise still surface of
the water. The stone cliffs tower high above you and look as featureless as
ever. To your east you can see where a trail has been drowned as the lake
emerges. It leads straight into the walls. You are veiled by the eerie shadow of
Celebdil.
name: The Festering Lake

desc:
You are at the south end of a festering lake. The water is thick and stagnant
which also makes it very smelly. All around you is barred with mountains except
to the north. You cannot tell how far the lake extends. You are veiled by the
eerie shadow of Celebdil.
name: The Festering Lake

desc:
You are at the south end of a long festering lake. The water is dark and smells
evil. You cannot see the southern edge of the lake, but to your east you can see
some tall grey-stone cliffs. In the north a clean mountain river joins the lake
but is soon swallowed up into the blackness. You are veiled by the eerie shadow
of Celebdil.
name: The Festering Lake

desc:
You can travel along a small track east-west made by mountain goats. It is
easier to walk upon than scree or heather. As you travel east you are climbing
further up the spurs and outcrops of Celebdil. You can see the great walls of
Moria down in a valley to your north east. They look grand and probably have
many stories to tell about wars of old.
name: Mountainous Terrain

desc:
The crack to the north is quite a squeeze and it leads out into the river
Sirannon. The water here is quiet and still, it leads up to a rocky beach. The
beach is generally narrow, but in the west end it leads to a deep cavern.
name: An Underground Beach

desc:
The cave ventures deep into the mountainside. Your light cannot fill the whole
cavern and there are always dark patches that may hide some danger.
name: A Deep Cavern

desc:
As you slide below the surface of the murky pool, your vision decreases to the
point of barely detecting your hand in front of your face. Light filters in from
the surface however, just enough to see a piece of ripped flesh go floating in
front of your eyes. Mixtures of animal excrement and decaying organic matter is
what gives the pool its murkiness. The water is cool, having just passed through
underground rivers, and there is an air of evil here. Long dim shapes sway
ominously a few feet away.
name: Underwater in the Festering Lake

desc:
The last tremulous gleam reflected by the surface of the lake momentarily dances
on the walls of the opening before the darkness swallows everything. The filth
that has for long poured inside the mountain has shaped this drifting tunnel.
Filled with a calmness, it slithers wide into the mountain. The sharp
foundation, which is surrounded by a cold, murky water, emerges each time the
tunnel narrows. The silence is unbroken with the exception of softly vibrating
debris that briefly stir the viscous excrement at the bottom.
name: Dark Tranquility

desc:
Natural rock forms a dark tunnel here, with jagged and sharp edges protruding
from the ceiling. The wide tunnel continues to the west, where it appears to
narrow and descend slightly. There are indentations in the rock along the
eastern tunnel wall above a pile of scattered debris, suggesting excavation that
was started but not finished.
name: Deformed Rock Tunnel

desc:
The jagged walls and uneven floor of this roughly-hewn tunnel suggest that it
was once a natural cavern. An effort to expand the cave to make passage easier
is evident, but the work is crude and incomplete. Water drips through cracks in
the ceiling above and gathers in a small, dirty puddle along the northern wall.
name: Deformed Tunnel Junction

desc:
This fort consists of a single, circular room formed by massive walls of natural
rock. The walls are rough and uneven, extending from the floor to the ceiling
only a short distance above; this lack of height contrasts with the breadth of
the cavern, creating an area that is at once spacious and cramped. Stairs carved
from the natural rock lead up to an opening in the ceiling.
name: Concealed Fort in the Rock

desc:
The walls here form a large, circular chamber. Narrow stairs extend out from the
walls, carved from the same stone, and allow passage up or down. The floor has
been smoothed, creating an even surface; the ceiling, which is jagged and
uneven, seems to have received less attention. Several racks holding crude
weapons line the walls. These racks, together with a row of mouldy blankets,
suggest that this chamber serves as the fort's barracks.
name: Barracks

desc:
The crown of the fort is a crude, small chamber. Narrow slits look out over the
hills below, and provide a perfect view of a crevice to the east. The slits are
hidden beneath an overhang on the outside and are unlikely to be visible from
the ground below. A set of stairs leads down into the fort. There is a
door-sized hole in the wall to the south which leads outside onto a ledge.
name: Crown of a Fort

desc:
This narrow ledge is only a couple of feet wide and less than five yards long.
It clings to a near-vertical rock face and does not look particularly stable. At
one end the ledge narrows until it disappears, while the other end leads to a
dark opening in the rock. A rocky crevice is visible beneath the ledge.
name: Small Ledge

desc:
The ground underfoot has been well-trod into nought but dust and mud. Little
grows here other than isolated tufts of grass. There is a simple rickety-looking
fence to the west, extending north and south to separate a tent camp on this
side of the fence from the surrounding fields. A gap in the fence, barely wide
enough to allow a wagon, allows access to the outside.
name: In an Encampment

desc:
The ground here is well-trampled and the grass has been churned to dirt and mud.
A rickety-looking fence to the west separates the inside of this camp from
grassy fields on the other side. There is a large tent to the north, and
numerous other tents to the east and south.
name: Well-trampled Path in a Camp

desc:
The grass has been beaten down and flattened by what looks to be numerous feet,
hooves, and cart wheels. A little pothole, probably created by removing a stone
that was damaging wagon wheels, lies in wait to trip up inobservant travellers.
Every few yards, paths branch off leading in the direction of tent entrances.
name: Path between Tents

desc:
This tent serves as a temporary shelter and stable for horses. Mounds of straw
sit against the western tent-wall. Cloth flaps separate the stalls of individual
horses. Saddles and other riding supplies hang from racks flanking the entrance
to the tent. The air is stale and filled with the odour of horses and
horse-dung.
name: Tent Stable

desc:
This floor is lined with haphazardly laid bedrolls overlapping in heaps.
Clearly, many sleepers occupy this tent, though there would be little room for
them to do anything else. Various pieces of cast-off equipment scattered around
the entrance suggest a martial purpose to this shelter.
name: Barracks Tent

desc:
There are several long, low tables with benches in the centre of this very large
tent. Along the north wall there are stone ovens and an open firepit. Several
sturdy spits are suspened over the fires, and several hanging carcasses roast in
the flames. Rows of wooden poles, fifteen feet tall, hold up the tent's ceiling.
name: Mess Tent

desc:
The grass here has been flattened by many boots and hoof prints. To the north, a
rickety-looking fence extends to the east and west, marking the borders of this
tent-camp. A narrow gap in the fence is the only exit from the camp here. Just
inside the gap, a heavy wooden stake with a T-shaped cross section has been
placed in the ground, perfect for securing animals.
name: In a Camp

desc:
This tent appears to serve many occupants as a home. Tattered bedding sits on
the floor in many sorry piles. The structure of the tent is in ill repair;
mismatched patches cover the many tears in the fabric. There is a gaping hole in
the roof and a simple fireplace in the form of a ring of stones on the ground
beneath it.
name: Hovel Tent

desc:
This is an intersection on the pathway going through the camp. A large tent is
visible to the south-east. The tent is made of high quality canvas that stands
out amongst the more crude tents surrounding it. It can probably protect the
inhabitants from harshest of storms.
name: Intersecting Paths

desc:
Surrounded by mud and torn up grass on all sides, a small clump of blooming
flowers has been left untouched. There are tents in every direction, but the one
to the east stands out amongst the rest. It is very large and while some of the
other tents look worn and threadbare, this one is very fine.
name: Before a Large Tent

desc:
The awning of this fortress of cloth sags voluptuously overhead. At the centre
of the abode, an intricately carved pole has been planted in the ground. It
towers above, supporting the heavy swaths of canvas that billow out forming the
sides of the pavilion. Through the folds, three yawning openings can be seen.
name: Spacious Tent

desc:
A layer of fine skins and furs covers the tent floor, making it soft despite the
hard ground beneath it. Many partitions along the wall to the south indicate
small quarters for those of lesser status. An ornate flap of fine, oiled skins
spanning the centre of the northern wall indicates a chamber belonging to
someone of high standing.
name: Grand Pavilion

desc:
A brightly lit, spacious chamber stretches to the limits of the canvas walls.
Straw and sawdust cover the floor. A long table, simple in design, yet well
polished and sturdily built takes up most of the space in the centre of the room
with long benches stretching along its sides. A chair much grander than the
other graces the head of the table with its presence.
name: Feasting Chamber

desc:
So encased in fabric are the walls that not a sound can be heard through them.
The air in here hangs desperately motionless, hot and heavy. Heaps of fine
linens and rugs swallow all but the most forceful of footsteps. The chamber is
lit by a single lamp, casting a warm glow about the room.
name: Receiving Chamber

desc:
Ostentatious wealth is writ in the decor of this chamber. Weapons both vicious
and ornamental line one wall, well-crafted armour and shields another. Novelties
of all sorts hang prominently from the ceiling: trophies, teeth, jewellery. A
mound of fine furs and hides sits in the centre of the chamber, and several
cushions face it in a circle.
name: Large Chamber

desc:
Every once in a while, a distant wolf howl breaks the idyllic scene set by this
grassy hill. The howls seem to come from the thick forest to your east. In every
other direction are fertile hills, covered by a sparse forest. Further west, the
small hills give way to much larger hills, ones appearing very rocky and jagged.
name: Woody Hills

desc:
Solemnity dominates this chamber, the smooth walls reflecting wavering light
from torches on the walls that strangely enough still burn after all these years
that must have passed since this place was in use. A spiraling stair leads up
through a slab to the natural crown above you.
name: A Waiting Room

desc:
A pale light enters through a tiny hole in the roof and penetrates through the
dust to light this huge hall. A grand table, plain and strong, stands almost as
long as the room itself with a noble chair at one end. A quick count shows that
over 50 dwarves could eat here simultanously.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
Just below the surface a large bed of pebbles threatens to ground your craft.
Without any large rocks to create turbulance the river moves swiftly over the
small rocks.
name: Bend in the River

desc:
You dug out enough mud to slide down into a hole, and now you can slide further
into what seems to be a large natural cavern. The fall is steep though, and you
would probably not get up again this way. You are stuck in a rather awkward
position at this point, so you better decide whether to get down or get out
quickly.
name: Entrance to the Natural Cavern
